title: Soil Water Holding Concepts
content: Soil texture affects how quickly fields dry and how much water can be stored in the root zone. Sandy soils tend to hold less plant-available water and may need more frequent checks. Clay soils can hold more water but are more vulnerable to waterlogging and slow infiltration. Loam is a moderate baseline. If soil type is unknown, Velia should lower confidence, avoid exact soil moisture claims, and ask for a topsoil or root-zone check.
